data.title: Utz Brands gives mixed Q4 results
data.description:
• Utz Brands press release (NYSE:UTZ): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.22 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $341M (-3.2% Y/Y) misses by $8.93M.
  • Organic Net Sales flat; Branded Salty Snacks increased 2.9%
  • Gross Profit Margin expansion of 230bps
  • Adjusted Gross Profit Margin expansion of 230bps
  • Net Income of $2.1 million
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 7.5% to $53.1 million
  • Adjusted Net Income increased 41.5% to $32.4 million
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share of $0.03
  • Adjusted Earnings Per Share increased 37.5% to $0.22

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Alibaba tops FQ3 expectations; Free cash flow plunges 31% Y/Y
Ivanhoe Mines GAAP EPS of $0.17, revenue of $40.82M; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Ivanhoe Mines press release (OTCQX:IVPAF): FY GAAP EPS of $0.17.
  • Revenue of $40.82M.
  • Ivanhoe Mines reports record $625M adjusted EBITDA in 2024, up from $604M in 2023.
  • Kamoa-Kakula delivers record copper production of 437,061 tonnes in 2024, including 133,819 tonnes in Q4.
  • Kamoa-Kakula maintains competitive cost structure: $1.71/lb Cost of Sales, $1.65/lb. cash cost (C1), meeting guidance for fourth consecutive year.
  • The Kamoa-Kakula Copper Complex sold 396,972 tonnes of payable copper in 2024 realizing revenue of $3,107 million for the Kamoa Holding joint venture, compared to 375,779 tonnes of payable copper sold for revenue of $2,704 million for the same period in 2023.
  • Outlook:
  • Kamoa-Kakula’s 2025 production guidance has been set at 520,000 to 580,000 tonnes of copper in concentrate. Kamoa-Kakula is targeting a production rate of approximately 600,000 tonnes of copper in concentrate for 2026, following the completion of power initiatives currently underway, together with optimization projects for improved Phase 1 and 2 recoveries (“Project 95”) and increased Phase 3 throughput underway.
    • Construction of Kamoa-Kakula’s 500,000-tonne-per-annum on-site, direct-to-blister copper smelter, the largest in Africa, is now complete. The ramp-up of the smelter complex has been deferred by up to three months due to power availability and is expected to commence in Q2 2025 and will drive improvement in margins.

data.title: LKQ Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80 beats by $0.06, revenue of $3.4B misses by $20M
data.description:
• LKQ press release (NASDAQ:LKQ): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $3.4B (-2.9% Y/Y) misses by $20M.
  • Fourth quarter organic revenue for parts and services decreased 3.6%; annual decreased 2.2%
  • Fourth quarter diluted EPS2 of $0.60; fourth quarter adjusted diluted EPS1,2 of $0.80
  • Annual diluted EPS2 of $2.62; annual adjusted diluted EPS1,2 of $3.48
  • Annual operating cash flow of $1.1 billion; free cash flow1 of $0.8 billion
  • Repurchased $80 million of LKQ shares in the fourth quarter of 2024
  • Exceeded commitment of returning 50% FCF to shareholders by returning over 80% in 2024 through share repurchases ($360 million) and cash dividends ($318 million)
2025 Full Year Outlook

Operating cash flow $1.075 to $1.275 billion

data.title: China’s iQIYI launches $300M convertible bond offering, shares dip
data.description:
iQIYI (NASDAQ:IQ) has launched an offering of US$300M aggregate principal amount of its convertible senior notes due 2030.

The notes will mature on March 15, 2030, unless repurchased, redeemed or converted in accordance with their terms prior to such date and will be senior, unsecured obligations of the company.

The Chinese video streaming platform will use the net proceeds from the sale for the repayment and/or repurchase of existing debt securities and general corporate purposes.

Shares of iQIYI (NASDAQ:IQ) were down 7.85% premarket.

data.title: Stock index futures slide after S&P 500 notches another record close
data.description:
Stock index futures slid on Thursday, a day after the S&P 500 closed at another record high, while investors geared up for more earnings reports.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.53%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.27%.

Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Wednesday, but the benchmark S&P 500 eventually closed at a record high as investors digested the Federal Reserve's latest minutes and President Donald Trump's latest tariff action.

"The minutes of the January Federal Reserve meeting gave a cautious policy outlook. Nervousness about the economic consequences of Trump’s policies was clear. In particular, the potential for Trump to ignite a further wave of inflation was cited," UBS' Paul Donovan saud.

"Markets put in a divergent performance over the last 24 hours, with U.S. assets continuing to reach fresh highs, even as the rest of the world struggled on the back of President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and concerns about Ukraine," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

On the earnings front, companies like Alibaba (BABA) and Walmart (WMT) are expected to report today.

The initial jobless claims will report before the opening bell today, and is expected to rise to 215K.

The February Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index will also land today and is anticipated to come in at 19.4.

The January leading index, which is being released during market hours, is expected to come in at -0.1% M/M.
